ralph-summary.context: This plan enhances the existing website design specification (docs/website-design-spec.md) with comprehensive details extracted from the source code. The existing document is well-structured but lacks depth in several critical areas. Based on thorough exploration of the codebase, 15 major sections need additional documentation to make the spec virtually document everything about this app.

scope-items.scope-item: major sections that need additional documentation
CLI flags with aliases, environment variables, hidden options
OpenCode providers with categories and authentication
prompt templates with variables and customization
supported languages with detection and review guidelines

step.title: Add complete CLI reference section
step.rationale: CLI flags are the primary interface for users and need comprehensive documentation

content.paragraph: Add new section 4.5.1 CLI Flags Reference containing preset flags, verbosity flags, agent selection flags, hidden/advanced flags, and all environment variables.
content.heading: Preset Flags (with exact values)
Verbosity Flags
Agent Selection Flags
Hidden/Advanced Flags
content.paragraph: Document all 25+ RALPH_* environment variables from source code.

step.title: Add complete configuration schema section
step.rationale: Configuration drives behavior and needs complete documentation with defaults and validation rules

content.paragraph: Add detailed documentation from src/config/unified.rs for GeneralConfig, CcsConfig, and FallbackConfig structs.
content.heading: GeneralConfig Struct
content.code-block: [general] verbosity = 2 # 0-4 (default: 2) interactive = true # keep agent in foreground isolation_mode = true # delete notes.md/issues.md at start auto_detect_stack = true # detect language for review guidelines checkpoint_enabled = true # enable checkpoint/resume strict_validation = false # strict prompt.md validation developer_iters = 5 # max 50, clamped if exceeded reviewer_reviews = 2 # max 10, clamped if exceeded developer_context = 1 # 0=minimal, 1=standard, 2=full reviewer_context = 0 # 0=minimal (fresh eyes) review_depth = "standard" # standard|comprehensive|security|incremental
content.heading: CcsConfig Struct
content.code-block: [ccs] output_flag = "--output-format=stream-json" verbose_flag = "--verbose" print_flag = "-p" # required for non-interactive mode yolo_flag = "--dangerously-skip-permissions" streaming_flag = "--include-partial-messages" json_parser = "claude" # always claude for ccs can_commit = true
content.heading: FallbackConfig Struct
content.code-block:
[agent_chain] developer = ["claude", "codex", "opencode"] reviewer = ["codex", "claude"] commit = ["claude", "codex"] # falls back to reviewer if empty max_retries = 3 # per agent before fallback retry_delay_ms = 1000 # base delay backoff_multiplier = 2.0 # exponential backoff max_backoff_ms = 60000 # 1 minute cap max_cycles = 3 # full chain cycles
[agent_chain.provider_fallback] opencode = ["-m opencode/glm-4.7-free", "-m opencode/claude-sonnet-4"]
content.heading: Validation Rules

list.item: empty string handling for flags (disables vs. keeps default)

step.title: Add all 18 built-in agents section
step.rationale: Users need to understand all available agents and their configurations

content.paragraph: Document all agents from examples/agents.toml organized by category.
content.heading: Premium/Primary Agents

list.item: claude - Claude Code (Anthropic): cmd=claude -p, output_flag=--output-format=stream-json, yolo_flag=--dangerously-skip-permissions, json_parser=claude, can_commit=true
ccs - Claude Code Switch: cmd=ccs, print_flag=-p (required), json_parser=ccs (maps to claude)
codex - OpenAI Codex CLI: cmd=codex exec, output_flag=--json, yolo_flag=--full-auto, json_parser=codex

content.heading: Mid-tier/Lower-cost Agents
Experimental/Specialized
Chat-only Agents (can_commit = false)
Dynamic Agent

step.title: Add complete OpenCode provider section
step.location: Expand Appendix B with all 45 providers
step.rationale: OpenCode supports many providers that need documentation

content.paragraph: Document all provider categories from src/agents/providers/types.rs.
content.heading: Provider Categories

row.cell: anthropic, openai, google, googlevertex, amazonbedrock, azureopenai, githubcopilot
groq, together, fireworks, cerebras, sambanova, deepinfra
deepseek, xai (grok), mistral, cohere, perplexity, ai21, veniceai
baseten, cortecs, scaleway, ovhcloud (ovh), ionet, nebius

content.heading: Authentication Requirements

step.title: Add complete workflow pipeline documentation
step.rationale: Understanding the pipeline is critical for users and maintainers

content.heading: Phase 1: Planning

list.item: Max 10 XSD retries for XML validation
Max 100 continuation loops for incomplete plans

content.heading: Phase 2: Development (per iteration)

list.item: Output: modified source files, git commit
Nested loops: outer (continuation max 100) + inner (XSD retry max 10)

content.heading: Phase 3: Review (per cycle)
content.paragraph: 3a. Review pass: produces .agent/issues.md with XML issues or no-issues-found. Early exit if no issues found. Context levels: universal, minimal, full.
3b. Fix pass: processes issues.md. Status: all_issues_addressed, issues_remain, no_issues_found. Baseline update after fixes.
content.heading: Phase 4: Commit
content.paragraph: Multi-strategy retry: 1) normal XML prompt (all agents), 2) simplified XML prompt (all agents), 3) last valid extraction, 4) hardcoded fallback: chore: automated commit
Diff truncation (agent-specific limits): GLM 100KB, Claude 300KB, default 200KB.
content.heading: State Transition Diagram
content.code-block: start -> planning -> development -> [commit if changes] | development (iterations 1-n) | review cycle 1 |-> no issues -> exit |-> issues -> fix pass -> [commit if changes] | review cycle 2...n | end

step.title: Add checkpoint/resume system documentation
step.rationale: Checkpoint system enables resumption and needs documentation

list.item: Validation: verify checksums, git state, config
Restoration: apply CLI args, generate new run_id
fail (default): stop at first validation error

step.title: Add complete prompt template documentation
step.rationale: Templates drive AI behavior and need comprehensive documentation

content.heading: All 31 Templates

list.item: Review (4 primary): standard_review, comprehensive_review, security_review, universal_review

content.heading: Template Variable Syntax
content.code-block: {{variable_name}} # simple substitution {{var|default="fallback"}} # with default {% if var %}...{% endif %} # conditionals {% for item in list %}...{% endfor %} # loops {{> partial/path}} # include partials {# comment #} # comments (stripped)
content.heading: Common Variables
Template Override System
content.paragraph: Priority: user templates (~/.config/ralph/templates/) greater than embedded. Override by creating ~/.config/ralph/templates/{name}.txt

step.title: Add language detection and guidelines section
step.location: New section 4.10 Language Detection and Review Guidelines
step.rationale: Language detection drives review guidelines selection

list.item: Compiled: rust, go, java, kotlin, c, c++, c#, swift, zig, nim, v, scala
Dynamic: python, ruby, php, lua, perl, r, elixir, haskell, ocaml, f#, clojure
Rust: actix, axum, rocket, tokio, warp, tauri, leptos, yew
JavaScript/TypeScript: react, vue, svelte, angular, next.js, nuxt, express, fastify, nestjs, electron

step.title: Add JSON parser system documentation
step.rationale: Parser system is critical for agent output handling

list.item: Health monitoring (greater than 50% parse errors triggers warning)

step.title: Add CCS integration details
step.rationale: CCS is a key integration that needs detailed documentation

content.heading: Profile Resolution Order
Environment Variable Loading

list.item: Loads from env object in settings file
Security: blocks dangerous vars (LD_PRELOAD, PATH, SHELL, etc.)
Value validation: no null bytes, newlines, backticks

content.heading: Direct Claude Bypass
content.paragraph: Conditions for bypass: 1) named alias (not empty), 2) successfully loaded env vars, 3) configured command targets profile, 4) claude binary found in PATH
content.heading: Fuzzy Profile Matching
Debug Mode

step.title: Add error handling and fallback documentation
step.location: New section 4.12 Error Handling and Fallback Chains
step.rationale: Error handling is critical for reliability

content.heading: Three Levels of Fallback
content.paragraph: Level 1: Agent-level - developer = ["claude", "codex", "opencode"]. Triggers: rate limits (429), auth failures, token exhaustion, command not found.
Level 3: Exponential backoff - delay = min(base * multiplier^cycle, max_backoff_ms). Example: 1000ms, 2000ms, 4000ms, ..., 60000ms cap.
content.heading: Error Classification

list.item: permanent: auth failures, command not found

step.title: Add work guide templates section
step.location: Expand section 4.9 or create new subsection
step.rationale: Work guides help users get started quickly

content.heading: All 20 Work Guides
Usage
content.code-block: ralph --init feature-spec # create prompt.md from template ralph --list-work-guides # list all available templates

step.title: Add troubleshooting matrix
step.rationale: Users need help diagnosing common issues

content.heading: Common Issues and Solutions
Diagnostic Commands
content.code-block: ralph --diagnose # system info and config ralph --list-available-agents # installed agents only ralph --dry-run # validate without running ralph --verbosity debug # maximum logging RALPH_CCS_DEBUG=1 ralph # CCS-specific debug

step.title: Add git integration details
step.rationale: Git integration is core functionality needing documentation

list.item: --with-rebase: enable automatic rebase before and after pipeline
State machine for complex rebase operations
Git config: git config user.name, git config user.email

step.title: Add .agent/ directory structure documentation
step.rationale: Directory structure needs documentation for debugging

content.code-block: .agent/ ├── prompt.md.backup # prompt.md integrity backup ├── plan.md # current plan (temporary) ├── issues.md # review issues (optional cleanup) ├── notes.md # agent notes ├── status.md # current status ├── commit-message.txt # generated commit message ├── checkpoint.json # checkpoint state (v3) ├── last_prompt.txt # last prompt sent ├── start_commit # baseline commit reference ├── review_baseline # review diff baseline └── logs/ ├── planning_{iter}_{agent}_{model}.log ├── developer_{iter}_{agent}_{model}.log ├── developer_{iter}_attempt_{retry}/ ├── reviewer_review_{j}_attempt_{retry}_{agent}_{model}.log ├── reviewer_fix_{j}_{agent}_{model}.log └── commit_generation/run_{timestamp}/ ├── attempt_001_*.log ├── attempt_001_parsing_trace.log └── summary.txt

risk-pair.risk: Document becoming outdated as code changes
risk-pair.mitigation: Reference source file paths in documentation so future maintainers know where to verify accuracy
Use clear section hierarchy, tables, and collapsible sections where appropriate

risk-pair.risk: Duplicating information already in existing docs
risk-pair.mitigation: Cross-reference existing docs (agent-compatibility.md, quick-reference.md) rather than duplicating

risk-pair.risk: Missing edge cases or hidden behaviors
risk-pair.mitigation: Source code exploration was thorough; marked areas of uncertainty where they exist

risk-pair.risk: Technical accuracy for OpenCode providers (external API)
risk-pair.mitigation: Provider list is from source code (providers/types.rs), not external API; models.dev integration is documented as dynamic

verification.expected-outcome: Every major claim can be traced to a specific file:line
Ensure no contradictions with docs/agent-compatibility.md, docs/quick-reference.md
